data.title: RED test: Write failing tests for directory scanning logic
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Description
Create tests/hooks/test-directory-monitor.sh with failing tests for the directory scanning function.

## Test Approach
- Given a temp git repo with directories containing known file counts (e.g., dir-a with 35 files, dir-b with 10 files), When _dm_scan_directories is called with threshold 30, Then only dir-a is returned as oversized
- Given directory_monitor.exclude_dirs includes dir-a, When _dm_scan_directories is called, Then dir-a is not flagged regardless of file count
- Given git ls-files returns paths with leading ./, When directories are counted, Then paths are normalized (no ./ prefix)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/hooks/test-directory-monitor.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/hooks/test-directory-monitor.sh
- [ ] Test file contains at least 3 test functions covering threshold, exclusion, and path normalization
  Verify: grep -c 'test_.*()' tests/hooks/test-directory-monitor.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] .test-index updated with RED marker for plugins/dso/hooks/lib/directory-monitor.sh
  Verify: grep '\[.*\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index | grep -q 'directory-monitor'
- [ ] Tests fail (RED) when run before implementation exists
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-directory-monitor.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'FAIL\|ERROR'
